reviewedFeatures.summary: The feature removed the round-1 double-load mismatch, but a hydrated in-progress assistant still never became the active ChatPanel stream, so seeded post-click deltas were buffered and dropped.

The follow-on ChatPanel change repaired the seeded `currentStreamId` handoff, but the selection path still accepts a synthesized replacement row when persistence lands after reconcile and before render, losing already-buffered pre-click text.

blockingIssues.description: Selecting a running bridge scoop can still render the earlier reconciled snapshot even if `OffscreenBridge.persistScoop()` commits the real in-progress assistant immediately afterward. In that interleaving `OffscreenClient.currentMessageId` stays empty, the next post-click `text_delta` synthesizes a replacement assistant id, the panel loses the bridge's already-buffered pre-click text, and `handleTurnEnd()` can later persist that truncated local row back over the fuller bridge state.
blockingIssues.suggestedFix: Make the selection handoff preserve the real in-progress assistant identity and content across the reconcile→render gap. If persistence lands after reconcile but before render, reconcile or refresh the rendered session before accepting post-click deltas, and tighten the regression to require the original hydrated message id/content instead of a synthesized replacement row.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated `.factory/library/architecture.md` to state that a selection-time fix is not complete unless the hydrated in-progress assistant remains the row that receives post-click deltas; a synthesized replacement row that drops pre-click buffered text is still a failure and can later overwrite fuller bridge state.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: For UI race-condition fixes, require regression tests to assert exact message identity/continuity and preserved pre-click content through the described interleaving; row-count-only assertions or synthesized replacement rows should not be accepted as success.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The misc-hydration `fix-unify-selection-session-read` feature described a race where the hydrated assistant must remain `msg-5`, but the committed `selection-session-race` test accepted a synthesized `scoop-<jid>-<uid>` row instead; round-2 scrutiny and the follow-on fix review both found the underlying continuity bug remained.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Strengthen the handoff rules so any skipped live CDP verification for UI-facing ChatPanel/offscreen-client fixes must set `followedProcedure: false` and call out the skip explicitly, even when the worker believes the change is an internal routing tweak.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: This milestone's `fix-chatpanel-adopt-seeded-streamid` handoff skipped manual verification for a user-visible chat-rendering fix yet still reported `followedProcedure: true`; a similar mismatch was already recorded in misc-hydration round 1, so the rule remains easy to misapply.
